summary: Kenneth demonstrates a covered call strategy on Ford stock held since 2008, selling 6 contracts at the $16 strike when implied volatility was elevated after a stock price surge. He earned $302 in premium while retaining shares, planning to repeat the process on any pullbacks until eventual assignment.
takeaways: Sell covered calls when IV is elevated after stock momentum (Ford jumped $12→$16)
Strike selection: sell at-the-money or slightly out-of-the-money strikes ($16 strike when stock at $16)
Monitor premium decay week-to-week: $302 in week 1 vs only $120 potential in week 2 after momentum fizzled
Covered calls allow profit on premium even if stock breaks even or declines slightly
Plan for eventual assignment but use interim periods to layer additional premium sales
6 contracts on several-hundred-share position provides reasonable capital efficiency

strategy_candidates.change: Establish IV-based entry triggers for covered call selling (e.g., only sell when IV rank >60%) to maximize premium collection on the wheel
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly notes he sold covered calls because 'options premiums would be really high' after stock ripped; he avoided selling the following week when premiums degraded to 20 cents, showing IV-dependent premium quality matters significantly to position profitability

title: Selling ford stock
